title: ci: cancel-in-progress cancels overlapping CI runs on main pushes
body:
## Problem

`ci.yml` sets `cancel-in-progress: true` for both pull requests and pushes to `main`:

```yaml
concurrency:
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true
```

When multiple commits land on `main` in quick succession, an in-flight CI run can be cancelled by a newer push to the same branch.

## Review context

Sandbox GHA migration review (mtrmac, PR #1) asked whether pushes to `main` should run to completion for every merge commit, so post-merge CI state is available for each one.

mtrmac noted no strong opinion on whether running CI on `main` pushes is valuable, but if it is, that implies not cancelling those runs.

Podman uses the same concurrency pattern.

## Related

- Sandbox PR #1 review thread
- `ci.yml` concurrency block
